<!--
Efficient Labs GitHub Operating Standard — every PR carries these seven H1
sections. The pr-discipline gate (where installed) blocks merge when they are
missing, when no context is linked, or when Proof / Receipts has no filled
bullets. Draft PRs are warned, not blocked.

Branch prefixes: feat/ fix/ docs/ chore/ refactor/ test/ ci/ security/ release/
(codex/ and seif/ are recognized machine-provenance prefixes).
NEVER rename the head branch of an open PR — GitHub auto-closes the PR.
-->

# Summary
<!-- One or two sentences: what this PR does and why, readable without the diff. -->

# Problem
<!-- What is broken/missing/risky today. Cite the incident, finding, or gap. -->

# Solution
<!-- How this PR fixes it. Call out the files/surfaces touched and any decisions made. -->

# User / Project Impact
<!-- Who/what changes when this merges. Be factual — no inflated claims. -->

# Linked Context
<!-- At least one real reference: Closes #N / Fixes #N / Refs #N, an ADR, a doc, or a decision. -->

# Proof / Receipts
<!-- FILLED bullets only — real commands with real results. e.g.:
- Tests (`<command>`): <n> pass / <n> fail (exit 0)
- CI run: <link>
- Receipt / checkpoint: `<hash>`
-->
-

# Merge Readiness
<!-- Honest state: what's verified, what's pending, who gates the merge.
Default posture: never auto-merged — founder is the merge gate. -->
